hints.dative: 'Ipsi' can be dative singular, but here it is nominative plural — subject of 'tacuerunt'
hints.genitive: Genitive singular would be 'ipsius' — 'ipsi' here is nominative plural
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'ipsis' — 'ipsi' is nominative plural

hints.to touch: 'To touch' is 'tangere' — 'tacuerunt' comes from 'tacere' (to be silent)
hints.to hide: 'To hide' is 'celare' — 'tacere' means to be silent
hints.to flee: 'To flee' is 'fugere' — 'tacere' means to be silent

steps.reveal: tacuerunt = they were silent / they kept silent

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'tacent' — 'tacuerunt' with -erunt is perfect
hints.imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'tacebant' — 'tacuerunt' is perfect indicative

hints.tango, tangere, tetigi, tactum: These are the parts of 'tangere' (to touch)
hints.tego, tegere, texi, tectum: These are the parts of 'tegere' (to cover)
hints.teneo, tenere, tenui, tentum: These are the parts of 'tenere' (to hold)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'nemo' — 'nemini' is dative singular
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'nemine' or 'nullo' — 'nemini' is dative

hints.to give: 'To give' is 'dare' — 'dicere' means to say or tell

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'dicunt' — 'dixerunt' with the dix- perfect stem is perfect
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'dicent' — 'dixerunt' is perfect indicative
hints.perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'dixerint' — 'dixerunt' is perfect indicative

hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the parts of 'ducere' (to lead)
hints.disco, discere, didici, —: These are the parts of 'discere' (to learn)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the parts of 'dare' (to give)

steps.reveal: illis diebus = in those days (abl. of time when)

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'illi dies' — 'illis diebus' is ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative of duration would be 'illos dies' — 'illis diebus' is ablative of time when
hints.dative: 'Illis diebus' could be dative, but here it is ablative of time when

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'diei' ends in -ei
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'diei' ends in -ei
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'diei' ends in -ei

steps.prompt: What is the narrative context: why did 'ipsi' keep silent?
steps.target: the disciples witnessed the Transfiguration and told no one at that time
choices: the disciples witnessed the Transfiguration and told no one at that time
they were afraid of the Pharisees
Jesus commanded silence after a healing
they did not understand the parable

hints.they were afraid of the Pharisees: Fear of Pharisees is not the context — the silence follows the extraordinary vision on the mountain
hints.Jesus commanded silence after a healing: While Jesus sometimes commands post-miracle silence, this is after the Transfiguration
hints.they did not understand the parable: No parable is involved — the disciples kept silent about seeing Moses and Elijah with Jesus

translation: And they themselves were silent and told no one in those days
